comment: EmulatR ES40 platform device manifest (Tsunami 21272, EV6, up to 4 CPU; boot uniprocessor with cpuCount=1). Created 2026-06-16 for the halt-button generalization test (es40cv_v7_3.exe). KNOWN DIVERGENCES the test will expose: (1) the REAL ES40 south bridge is the ALi M1543C, NOT the Cypress CY82C693 modeled here -- the Cypress is a STAND-IN so the ISA/UART console path works; the ES40 SRM probes the ALi at its own BDF/config and may diverge during south-bridge init. (2) ES40 is dual-Pchip (hose 1 populated); EmulatR models Pchip1 as a coarse all-ones mirror. (3) ES40 boots on-board Symbios/QLogic SCSI, not IDE; the IDE/ATAPI here is a DS10-style stand-in. (4) PCI slot/BDF topology is _PROVISIONAL (DS10-derived) -- exact ES40 BDF needs an ES40 PCICFG trace. The halt-button check itself is chipset-level (TIG smir, TIG+0x40), independent of the above, which is why this manifest is sufficient to reach the halt stage. Strict JSON: no // or /* */ comments -- use comment fields.

pci_devices.name: isa_bridge
pci_devices.comment: STAND-IN: real ES40 south bridge is ALi M1543C (vendor 0x10b9, device 0x1533 bridge / 0x1533+0x1543 funcs). EmulatR has no ALi model yet; the Cypress stand-in carries the ISA/UART/PIC console path at standard ports.

pci_devices.name: ide
pci_devices.comment: STAND-IN IDE/ATAPI (DS10-style). ES40 boots on-board SCSI; this carries dqa0/dqa1 for parity with the DS10 harness.

storage.comment: primary master = dqa0. media = bare image FILENAME resolved against [Storage] diskDir; empty = no disk.
primary slave = dqa1, ATAPI CD. media empty = no disc loaded.
